BASE.name: ATTACK_VECTOR
BASE.justification: Unescaped attributes can be influenced through web inputs and rendered to users.

BASE.name: PRIVILEGES_REQUIRED
BASE.justification: Often exploitable without authentication, depending on page exposure.

BASE.name: USER_INTERACTION
BASE.justification: Victim must load the vulnerable page.

BASE.name: SCOPE
BASE.justification: Primarily impacts the same application scope in the client context.

BASE.name: CONFIDENTIALITY
BASE.justification: Potential limited exposure, such as access to page-level data or tokens.

BASE.name: INTEGRITY
BASE.justification: Injected attributes can alter client-side behavior or content presentation.

TEMPORAL.name: EXPLOIT_CODE_MATURITY
TEMPORAL.justification: The vulnerability can be reliably exploited using well-known and commonly available techniques.

TEMPORAL.name: REMEDIATION_LEVEL
TEMPORAL.justification: Proper output encoding using recommended functions effectively mitigates the issue.

TEMPORAL.name: REPORT_CONFIDENCE
TEMPORAL.justification: The detection logic reliably identifies unescaped attribute contexts.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_ATTACK_VECTOR
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The affected page is accessed remotely over the network, allowing exploitation through standard HTTP requests.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_ATTACK_COMPLEXITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Environmental conditions do not significantly increase the difficulty of exploiting unescaped attributes.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_PRIVILEGES_REQUIRED
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Attackers do not need authentication or elevated privileges if the vulnerable page is reachable.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_USER_INTERACTION
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: A user must load the affected page for the injected attribute payload to execute in the browser.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_SCOPE
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The impact remains within the same application and browser execution context.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_CONFIDENTIALITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Unescaped attributes may expose limited client-side information but do not directly leak backend data.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_INTEGRITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Injected attributes can modify UI behavior or presentation without directly altering server-side data.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_AVAILABILITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The issue does not affect application uptime or availability.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: CONFIDENTIALITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Protecting UI-displayed data and session context is important but not typically mission-critical.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: INTEGRITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Maintaining UI correctness and preventing client-side manipulation is important for user trust.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: AVAILABILITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Availability is not a primary concern for this client-side injection vulnerability.
